channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Steam Controller Reservations Now Backed Into 2027
item.content:encoded:
It was only last month that the Valve launched the newest iteration of the Steam Controller to much fanfare, with demand rapidly exceeding Valve's expectations. It was to such a degree that they moved to a reservation based queue as demand was out stripping supply, and now deliveries for newly reserved Steam Controllers are set for 2027.

If you go to the Steam Controller page, you can see the estimated time frame for when you might get your Steam Controller based on the reservations update. The time windows are:

  • By September 2026
  • By December 2026
  • 2027

If you go and place a reservation today you'll see that your reservation is set for 2027, with future dates and details to come. If you previously had reserved a Steam Controller, when you go to the Steam Controller page, you'll see there what delivery bucket your order falls into.

Reservations for Steam Controllers are still open, but its worth noting it is going to be at least a 6 month wait to get yours if you were to place a reservation today. If you have a reservation, or are going to place one, once placed wait until you get an email for your spot in the queue. Once you come to the front of the queue, you will have 72 hours to purchase your Steam Controller, or else you'll be removed from the list, and they'll go to the next person in line. You can only reserve one Steam Controller per user, and if you've purchased one they haven't opened it up yet to being purchased by others as far as I know.

As far as other Steam Hardware like the Steam Machine, and Steam Frame, we're still waiting on additional information as Valve has continued to delay posting prices and information on them as they hope some of the economic pressures on computer components layoff, letting them offer a better rate.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
